title: Rich Hearty Greek Noodles Pastitsio
description: A fatty and comforting Greek dish featuring narutomaki and double-fry KFC, perfect for a sick day. This Mediterranean-friendly recipe is formed into a pastitsio shape.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over medium heat and cook the ground beef until browned.
Add the narutomaki and cooked KFC chicken to the pan and stir to combine.
In a separate saucepan, heat the tomato sauce over low heat.
In another saucepan, heat the bechamel sauce over low heat.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked noodles, meat mixture, and tomato sauce, and stir to combine.
In a greased 9x13 inch baking dish, create a layer of the noodle mixture.
Pour the bechamel sauce over the noodle layer and top with parmesan cheese.
Repeat the layers one more time, ending with a layer of cheese on top.
Bake the pastitsio in the preheated oven for 30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
